response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has an impressively deep technical background spanning 30+ years, with genuine developer tooling experience (CI/CD infrastructure, test frameworks, automated testing courses), and community leadership through workshops and open-source projects. However, the role is explicitly a full-time people-leadership position requiring at least 2 years of direct people management, and the resume shows no evidence of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, hiring, or running a team. All titles across a long career remain individual contributor roles ('Developer', 'Staff Engineer', 'Founder'). The 'Staff' designations and community teaching show technical influence but not organizational people management, making him a poor fit for a Level II Manager role.
strengths: Extensive developer tooling experience: CI/CD setup, test runners (Trial), test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts), Selenium course creation, and MCP/agentic tooling
30+ years of broad technical depth across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — directly relevant to the engineering productivity focus
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing and coaching habits: organized Refactoring Dojos, Code Katas, delivered workshops, and gave conference talks
concerns: Zero evidence of direct people management: no mentions of direct reports, performance reviews, hiring, or team headcount across any role
No management-track titles or progression — every position from 1992 to present is an individual contributor role, including 'Staff' designations
No visible experience with organizational change leadership, roadmap ownership at a team level, or cross-team alignment — all preferred/required for this role
